title: Who Owns Your Health App Data?
subtitle: 你的健康App数据到底归谁？隐私保护的灰色地带

paragraphs.en: If you look at the apps on your phone, chances are you have at least one related to your health — and probably several. Whether it is a mental health app, a fitness tracker, a connected health device or something else, many of us are taking advantage of this technology to keep better track of our health in some shape or form. Recent research from the Organization for the Review of Care and Health Applications found that 350,000 health apps were available on the market, 90,000 of which launched in 2020 alone.
paragraphs.zh: 看一眼你手机里的应用程序，很可能至少有一个与健康相关——说不定还有好几个。无论是心理健康应用、健身追踪器、联网健康设备还是其他什么，我们许多人都在利用这类技术，以某种方式更好地管理自身健康。护理与健康应用审查组织的最新研究发现，市场上有35万款健康应用，其中仅2020年一年就上线了9万款。

paragraphs.en: While these apps have a great deal to offer, it is not always clear how the personal information we input is collected, safeguarded and shared online. Existing health privacy law, such as the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act, is primarily focused on the way hospitals, doctors' offices, clinics and insurance companies store health records online. The health information these apps and health data tracking wearables are collecting typically do not receive the same legal protections.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管这些应用能提供诸多便利，但我们输入的个人信息究竟是如何被收集、保护和在线共享的，往往并不透明。现行的健康隐私法律，例如《健康保险流通与责任法案》，主要规范的是医院、诊所、医生办公室和保险公司在线存储健康记录的方式。而这些健康应用和健康数据追踪可穿戴设备所收集的健康信息，通常并不受同等程度的法律保护。

paragraphs.en: Without additional protections in place, companies may share (and potentially monetize) personal health information in a way consumers may not have authorized or anticipated. In 2021, Flo Health faced a Federal Trade Commission investigation. The FTC alleged in a complaint that "despite express privacy claims, the company took control of users' sensitive fertility data and shared it with third parties."
paragraphs.zh: 在缺乏额外保护措施的情况下，公司可能会以消费者未曾授权或未曾预料到的方式共享（甚至可能变现）个人健康信息。2021年，Flo Health就曾面临美国联邦贸易委员会的调查。联邦贸易委员会在一份投诉中称，“尽管该公司明确宣称保护隐私，却控制用户的敏感生育数据并将其分享给第三方。”

paragraphs.en: Flo Health and the FTC settled the matter with a Consent Order requiring the company to get app users' express affirmative consent before sharing their health information as well as to instruct the third parties to delete the data they had obtained. Section 5 of the FTC Act empowers the FTC to initiate enforcement action against unfair or deceptive acts, meaning the FTC can only act after the fact if a company's privacy practices are misleading or cause unjustified consumer harm.
paragraphs.zh: Flo Health与联邦贸易委员会通过一项同意令达成和解，要求该公司在分享用户健康信息前必须获得用户的明确同意，并指示第三方删除已获取的数据。《联邦贸易委员会法案》第5条授权委员会对不公平或欺骗性行为发起执法行动，但这意味着联邦贸易委员会只能在事后采取行动——即当公司的隐私做法具有误导性或对消费者造成不当损害时。

paragraphs.en: While the FTC is doing what it can to ensure apps are keeping their promises to consumers around the handling of their sensitive health information, the rate at which these health apps are hitting the market demonstrates just how immense of a challenge this is. As to the prospects for federal legislation, commentators suggest that comprehensive federal privacy legislation seems unlikely in the short term. States have begun implementing their own solutions to shore up protections for consumer-generated health data.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管联邦贸易委员会正尽其所能确保应用程序在处理敏感健康信息方面信守对消费者的承诺，但这些健康应用涌入市场的速度本身就说明了挑战之巨大。至于联邦立法的前景，评论人士认为短期内不太可能出台全面的联邦隐私法。各州已开始着手实施各自的方案，以加强对消费者生成的健康数据的保护。

paragraphs.en: California has been at the forefront of state privacy efforts with the California Consumer Privacy Act of 2018. Virginia, Colorado and Utah have also recently passed state consumer data privacy legislation.
paragraphs.zh: 加利福尼亚州凭借2018年《加州消费者隐私法案》走在了各州隐私保护努力的最前沿。弗吉尼亚州、科罗拉多州和犹他州近期也通过了各自的州级消费者数据隐私立法。
